behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam between PR-change discovery and file-analysis dispatcher. MOCK-MISS: the failure appears when changed-file list handoff to analyzer is wrong (e.g., analyzer receives repo-wide file set). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required to reproduce this; an in-process fake changed-file provider is sufficient. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no. REJECTION-GATE: System was rejected because behavior text only requires correct changed-file filtering, not real GitHub API/network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real change-selection module to the real analysis dispatcher with an in-memory PR file list containing changed and unchanged paths; assert analyzer is invoked only for changed paths and never for unchanged paths.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam between findings aggregation, severity grouping, and comment rendering/posting payload creation. MOCK-MISS: a real failure is mis-wiring between aggregator and formatter (correct raw findings, wrong grouped output text). REAL-INFRA: real GitHub HTTP is not required to catch grouping defects; validating generated comment payload catches the failure. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no. REJECTION-GATE: System was rejected because behavior text targets grouping in comment content, not network delivery semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run aggregator + comment formatter together on mixed-severity findings and verify produced PR comment body contains distinct error/warning/info sections with the right findings under each section.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: environment seam (runtime performance under CI runner constraints, including process execution and filesystem/workload behavior). REAL-INFRA: timeout behavior is tied to real GitHub Actions execution environment and wall-clock runtime; in-process mocks hide true latency and resource contention. MOCK-HIDE: mocked linter/runtime would likely pass while real runner times out. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was rejected because behavior text explicitly requires meeting the 10-minute Actions timeout, which is an environment-level constraint.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the lint step end-to-end in CI-equivalent conditions on a synthetic PR-sized corpus of 200 files, measure wall-clock duration, and fail if total runtime exceeds 10 minutes.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: environment seam at subprocess boundary (real linter crash/exit behavior) plus continuation logic. REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly says 'linter process crashes'; true crash semantics (exit code, signal, stderr, partial output) are subprocess-level. MOCK-HIDE: replacing crash with a normal exception can miss supervisor/retry/continuation bugs. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) yes -> System, (b) no, (c) no. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was rejected because concrete behavior text requires handling an actual process crash, not just function error returns.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline against a fixture set where one file deterministically crashes the real linter subprocess; verify the step records crash for that file, continues analyzing remaining files, and posts a final PR comment with surviving findings.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: local function/object seam in deduplication logic (keying by file/line/rule or canonical fingerprint). MOCK-MISS: this behavior is primarily algorithmic and reproducible with direct inputs to dedupe function/object. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess dependence. LLM-DEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered but rejected because behavior text is about deterministic dedupe of overlapping rule outputs, which can be validated at the local dedupe boundary without module/environment coupling.
behaviors.test_description: Call deduplication logic with a fixture list containing intentional same-file same-line overlaps from different rules and assert output contains one canonical finding per dedupe key while preserving unique findings.
